history.content:
<thought>
I am at the bottom center and facing forward (upwards on the map). The cell directly ahead is a wall (#), which I cannot pass through. Therefore, moving forward is not possible. On my left and right, there are unknown or inaccessible areas. The safest course of action is to turn right to explore a possible path on my right side where there's floor (.) cells visible.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing right, and the cell directly ahead is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. The cells to the left contain lava (L), which is deadly, so turning left isn't safe. To find a way forward, I should turn left twice to face upward, where I can potentially explore the path with floor cells (.) above.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I have turned left and now face upward. The cell ahead is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. The next best option is to turn left again to face left, where cells are unknown but potentially accessible. Turning left will help me explore new terrain and avoid the walls and lava ahead.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing left. The cell ahead is a wall (#), so moving forward is not possible. To find a clear path, I will turn left again to face down. This direction appears more open based on previous observations and may provide a route to explore further safely.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing down. The cell ahead is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. Turning right to face left might provide an open path since there are some floor cells (.) to the left. I will turn right to face left and check for a safe cell to move forward next.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
After turning right, I am now facing left again. The cell in front of me is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. The previous observation showed floor cells (.) behind to my left and right after turning left from facing left. Because moving forward is blocked, I should try turning left to face up to explore new possibilities.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing forward (up the map). The cell ahead is a floor cell (.) which is safe to move into. Moving forward will help me explore more of the map and potentially find a path towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The observation appears the same as before the last move forward, indicating I have not moved yet or the view hasn't changed. I am facing forward with a floor (.) in front that is safe to move into. I will move forward to explore further.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The observation has not changed again, possibly indicating an issue or that I am blocked from moving forward. The cell in front is still floor (.) but the right and left cells are walls or unknown. To find an alternate path, I will turn left to face towards column C and see whether that direction is open.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I have turned left and now face column C. The cell in front of me (C7) contains lava (L), which is deadly. I must not move forward. To find a safer path, I will turn right to face my original direction, column D.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am back to facing forward at column D with the floor cell (D7) in front and a wall on the right (E7). Since the path forward is clear, I should move forward to advance.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am still facing forward at D7 with a floor cell in front. I will move forward again to continue progressing forward towards potential open space.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The observation did not update after the last move forward, suggesting I may be blocked or not advanced. The cell in front (D7) is still floor, so I will try turning right to explore possible other directions since the right side might open a path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing right at D7. The cell directly in front (E7) is lava, which is deadly. I need to avoid moving forward into lava. Looking left or backward might be better options. Since the path behind me might be blocked by walls, I will turn left to explore other directions and avoid the lava ahead.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
After turning left, the view back at D7 shows floor directly ahead but a wall at E7. I am currently facing left. The only safe move forward is onto D7, the floor cell ahead. I will move forward to proceed safely.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The observation did not change after my move, meaning I am still at D7 facing left. The path in front is clear floor; so I can safely move forward again to approach possible open space.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The observation is still the same after moving forward twice, indicating I haven't advanced or the local view is limited and unchanged. Since moving forward didn't bring me closer to a goal or new path, it is better to turn to explore another direction and find a way around the walls.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
After turning right, I now see that the cell in front of me (D7) is a free floor cell, with lava on the side (E7). Moving forward is safe and allows further exploration beyond the walls visible previously.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD

<thought>
The view did not change after moving forward, meaning I am likely blocked or unable to proceed further. Considering the lava on the right side and wall cells ahead, it is safer to turn again to explore a new direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT

<thought>
Now that I am facing towards column D, I see a wall column (C) running vertically on my left side, but ahead (D7) is a free cell, and further cells ahead are also free with some lava further off to the right. I should move forward to continue exploring this free path and avoid lava cells.
</thought>
<action>

</action>
